title: triticum repens subsp. glaucum
intro: Triticum repens subsp. glaucum, commonly known as wild rye or glaucous wheatgrass, is a subspecies of the perennial grass species Triticum repens, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Triticum repens subsp. glaucum include its potential to support healthy digestion, reduce stress, and improve respiratory health. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, powder, or supplement, making it a versatile option for those looking to incorporate its benefits into their daily routine.
